name: Pergamon Altar
description: The Pergamon Altar, also known as the Great Altar of Pergamon, was a monumental construction built in the ancient Greek city of Pergamon during the reign of King Eumenes II in the 2nd century BC. Dedicated to Zeus and Athena, it is a massive U-shaped structure adorned with intricate friezes depicting the Gigantomachy, the mythological battle between the Olympian gods and the Giants. The altar served as a significant religious and cultural center, showcasing the power and artistic prowess of the Attalid dynasty. The detailed sculptures vividly portray the intense struggle between order and chaos, reflecting the Greek worldview and their belief in the supremacy of the gods. The Altar's elaborate design and symbolic imagery reinforced the divine authority of the rulers and the city's importance in the Hellenistic world. Its relocation to Berlin in the late 19th century made it a centerpiece of the Pergamon Museum, ensuring its enduring legacy as a masterpiece of Hellenistic art and a powerful symbol of Greek mythology. The sheer scale and artistic quality cemented its place as a vital historical and mythological landmark, offering insights into the religious beliefs and artistic achievements of ancient Pergamon.
subtitle: Monumental Altar of Gods and Giants

keyMyths.title: The Gigantomachy
keyMyths.description: The Pergamon Altar depicts the Gigantomachy, a cosmic battle between the Olympian gods and the Giants. This myth represents the triumph of order and civilization over chaos and barbarism, reinforcing the power and authority of the gods.

keyMyths.title: Dedication to Zeus and Athena
keyMyths.description: The altar's dedication to Zeus, king of the gods, and Athena, goddess of wisdom and warfare, highlights Pergamon's reverence for these deities. The choice of these gods reflects the city's aspirations for power, prosperity, and intellectual achievement.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides a foundational account of the Gigantomachy and the Olympian gods.
sources.text: Provides a foundational account of the Gigantomachy and the Olympian gods.

sources.author: Hans Schrader
sources.description: A comprehensive study of Pergamon, its history, architecture, and archaeological discoveries.
